behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local scoring logic (token counting + threshold comparison) inside the keyword analyzer. No real I/O or model behavior is required to expose wrong sentiment assignment here.
behaviors.test_description: Call the keyword analyzer function with in-memory positive/negative dictionaries and thresholds, using reviews composed only of configured keywords; assert exact sentiment outputs across positive-only, negative-only, and neutral-count cases.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about explanation quality grounded in source text. If the explanation analyzer is model-driven, mocked outputs would hide grounding failures; correctness depends on real generation behavior, which can vary run-to-run.
behaviors.test_description: Send real reviews to the live explanation analyzer and assert the generated explanation includes or clearly cites concrete phrases from each review (not stock wording), with repeated runs to detect drift toward boilerplate.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: The failure is in deterministic tie-break logic within the keyword-frequency analyzer given configured thresholds/rules. It does not inherently require module wiring or external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the analyzer with controlled mixed-keyword inputs that produce ties (or near-ties) under test configs, and assert the exact configured tie-break outcome for each rule variant.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: Detecting sarcasm/intent is model-quality behavior, not simple deterministic parsing. Mocking would miss real misclassification and nuance failures; real model inference is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Run a sarcasm-focused review set through the real explanation analyzer and verify explanations explicitly interpret negative intent (despite positive tokens), tracking pass rate across multiple runs to capture non-deterministic misses.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: This combines module wiring (single response envelope) with semantic consistency between deterministic score and generated explanation. Using mocked explanation text can hide real direction mismatches from the live analyzer.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real end-to-end response path and assert: (1) score and explanation are both present in one payload, and (2) explanation sentiment direction matches the computed score across positive/negative/neutral cases.
